subject: Mathematics
title: Euclid's Elements
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Euclid's Elements.

hook.text: One book dominated mathematics for over 2,000 years. It wasn't written by a king or prophet.

buildup.text: Around 300 BC, Euclid compiled the Elements: 13 books covering geometry and number theory.

discovery.text: Starting from just five postulates, Euclid built 465 propositions through pure logic.

twist.text: It's the second-most printed book in history, after the Bible.

climax.text: Abraham Lincoln taught himself logic by studying Euclid's proofs by candlelight.

punchline.text: Five assumptions, 465 truths, and two millennia of influence.

quiz.question: How many postulates did Euclid start with in the Elements?
